# Codex Chrome Bridge

> Local Chrome extension, CLI, and MCP server for read-mostly control of a real Google Chrome profile from Codex-style agents.

Codex Chrome Bridge helps AI agents work with the user's own logged-in Chrome session while staying scoped to a dedicated Chrome tab group and requiring explicit confirmation for sensitive actions.

It exposes read-first browser automation through CLI and MCP tools including tab adoption, workspace policy status, a local command catalog, observe/find-elements, structured extraction, screenshots, PDF export, safe debug bundles, and confirmed interaction helpers.

## Best for

- MCP browser automation with a real Chrome profile.
- Codex-style AI agents that need local browser tools.
- Logged-in dashboard inspection for analytics, webmaster tools, admin panels, and extension-authenticated pages.
- Human-in-the-loop browser workflows where the user can confirm sensitive actions.
- Privacy-aware local browser control through a Chrome Manifest V3 extension, CLI, MCP server, and explicit workspace policy.

## Not for

- CAPTCHA bypass or anti-bot circumvention.
- Remote browser takeover.
- Credential extraction or secret harvesting.
- Unattended account mutation.
- Scraping private or access-controlled content without permission.

## Key docs

- `README.md` - overview, quick start, MCP setup, and safety model.
- `docs/MCP.md` - MCP server and tool reference.
- `docs/CLI.md` - command-line reference.
- `docs/EXTENSION.md` - Chrome extension setup.
- `docs/SAFETY.md` - privacy, confirmations, and sensitive-data rules.
- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` - system architecture.

## Discovery phrases

Codex Chrome Bridge, Chrome MCP server, Chrome extension MCP bridge, MCP browser automation, AI agent browser tools, real Chrome profile automation, logged-in Chrome automation, local-first browser automation, privacy-aware browser automation, Codex browser control, human-in-the-loop browser automation, browser observe tools, structured browser extraction, Chrome workspace policy.
